records.currentAuthority: route teamName plus body runId/memberName/runtimeSessionId; lane is server-resolved, but no route-specific machine credential is checked
records.idempotency: derived commandId; durable session evidence treats same session as duplicate and rejects a conflicting session

records.currentAuthority: body chooses source and user/member/cross-team target; current-run tombstone is checked, but canonical sender/target authorization is not credential-derived
records.idempotency: persisted key plus payload hash; duplicate resumes/verifies destination and conflicting reuse is rejected

records.currentAuthority: body selects member/task/event; lane and current run are checked but task mutation authorization is not credential-derived
records.idempotency: key is required and included in commandId; underlying attribution upsert is durable, but normalized-intent conflict evidence is not present at this boundary

records.currentAuthority: body run/member/session plus server-resolved lane and current-run tombstone check; no lane credential
records.idempotency: commandId includes observedAt; liveness snapshot write is repeatable, but no persisted monotonic sequence is enforced

records.caller: legacy OpenCode compatibility callback (and any browser able to reach the same route surface)
records.currentAuthority: body supplies decision, cwd, member and expectedMembers; write fence is in-memory and route has no machine/operator authority split
records.idempotency: derived from requestId+decision; bridge command ledger may recover the provider effect, but ingress claim is not durably persisted first
persistedEvidence: runtime approval/bridge command evidence when available
RuntimePermissionAnswered event only if an event sink is configured

trustSurfaceProof.currentNegativeControl: All five callbacks remain under /api/teams and register beside browser/operator routes; source contains no /api/runtime/v1 route or lane credential check.
trustSurfaceProof.targetBrowserAuthority: /api/hosted/v1/* uses operator session plus CSRF/Origin and cannot present runtime credentials.
trustSurfaceProof.targetRuntimeAuthority: /api/runtime/v1/runs/:runId/* uses only hash-verified lane/run-scoped relay credentials, ignores browser session/CSRF semantics, and cannot invoke operator verbs.
trustSurfaceProof.targetProviderExposure: Provider process receives a narrow local relay, never the canonical bearer in argv, env, settings, MCP config or run files.
